stem: A energy power grid uses 3 parallel generators for redundancy. The power maintained requires at least one generator to function. generator 1 has a reliability of 81%. generator 2 has a reliability of 77%. generator 3 has a reliability between 78% and 91% (uncertain due to limited operational data). What is the overall probability that the power maintained?

ground_truth.answer: P(power maintained) ranges from 0.9904 to 0.9961. Point estimate: 0.993.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 generators fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.19 × 0.23 × 0.22 = 0.0096. P(works) = 0.9904.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.19 × 0.23 × 0.09 = 0.0039. P(works) = 0.9961.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
